title: श्री गंगा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गंगा स्तोत्रम् ॥

देवि सुरेश्वरि भगवति गङ्गे त्रिभुवनतारिणि तरलतरङ्गे।
शङ्करमौलिविहारिणि विमले मम मतिरास्तां तव पदकमले॥१॥

भागीरथि सुखदायिनि मातस्तव जलमहिमा निगमे ख्यातः।
नाहं जाने तव महिमानं पाहि कृपामयि मामज्ञानम्॥२॥

हरिपदपाद्यतरङ्गिणि गङ्गे हिमविधुमुक्ताधवलतरङ्गे।
दूरीकुरु मम दुष्कृतिभारं कुरु कृपया भवसागरपारम्॥३॥

तव जलममलं येन निपीतं परमपदं खलु तेन गृहीतम्।
मातर्गङ्गे त्वयि यो भक्तः किल तं द्रष्टुं न यमः शक्तः॥४॥

पतितोद्धारिणि जाह्नवि गङ्गे खण्डितगिरिवरमण्डितभङ्गे।
भीष्मजननि हे मुनिवरकन्ये पतितनिवारिणि त्रिभुवनधन्ये॥५॥

कल्पलतामिव फलदां लोके प्रणमति यस्त्वां न पतति शोके।
पारावारविहारिणि गङ्गे विमुखयुवतीकृततरलापाङ्गे॥६॥

तव चेन्मातः स्रोतःस्नातः पुनरपि जठरे सोऽपि न जातः।
नरकनिवारिणि जाह्नवि गङ्गे कलुषविनाशिनि महिमोत्तुङ्गे॥७॥

पुनरसदङ्गे पुण्यतरङ्गे जय जय जाह्नवि करुणापाङ्गे।
इन्द्रमुकुटमणिराजितचरणे सुखदे शुभदे भृत्यशरण्ये॥८॥

रोगं शोकं तापं पापं हर मे भगवति कुमतिकलापम्।
त्रिभुवनसारे वसुधाहारे त्वमसि गतिर्मम खलु संसारे॥९॥

अलकानन्दे परमानन्दे कुरु करुणामयि कातरवन्द्ये।
तव तटनिकटे यस्य निवासः खलु वैकुण्ठे तस्य निवासः॥१०॥

वरमिह नीरे कमठो मीनः किं वा तीरे शरटः क्षीणः।
अथवा श्वपचो मलिनो दीनस्तव न हि दूरे नृपतिकुलीनः॥११॥

भो भुवनेश्वरि पुण्ये धन्ये देवि द्रवमयि मुनिवरकन्ये।
गङ्गास्तवमिदममलं नित्यं पठति नरो यः स जयति सत्यम्॥१२॥

येषां हृदये गङ्गाभक्तिस्तेषां भवति सदा सुखमुक्तिः।
मधुराकान्ता पञ्झटिकाभिः परमानन्दकलितललिताभिः॥

गङ्गास्तोत्रमिदं भवसारं वाञ्छितफलदं विदितमुदारम्।
शङ्कराचार्यरचितं पठन्ति सज्जनैर्मनसि ये धृतम्॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छङ्कराचार्यविरचितं गङ्गास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganga Stotram ||

Devi Sureshvari Bhagavati Gange Tribhuvanatarini Taralatrange.
Shankaramauliviharini Vimale Mama Matiraastaam Tava Padakamale.||1||

Bhagirathi Sukhadayini Mataastava Jalamahima Nigame Khyaatah.
Naaham Jaane Tava Mahimaanam Paahi Kripaamayi Maamajnaanam.||2||

Haripadapadyatarangini Gange Himavidhumuk​taadhavalatarange.
Doorikuru Mama Dushkritibhaaram Kuru Kripayaa Bhavasaagarapaaram.||3||

Tava Jalamamalam Yena Nipeetam Paramapadam Khalu Tena Griheetam.
Matargange Tvayi Yo Bhaktah Kila Tam Drashtum Na Yamah Shaktah.||4||

Patitoddhaarini Jahnavi Gange Khanditagirivaramanditabhange.
Bheeshmajanani He Munivarakanye Patitanivarini Tribhuvanadhanya.||5||

Kalpalataativa Phaladam Loke Pranamati Yastvaam Na Patati Shoke.
Paaraaviharini Gange Vimukhayuvatikritataralaapaange.||6||

Tava Chenmaatah Srotahsnaatah Punarapi Jathare So'pi Na Jaatah.
Narakanivarini Jahnavi Gange Kalushavinashini Mahimottunge.||7||

Punarasadange Punyatarange Jaya Jaya Jahnavi Karunaapaange.
Indramukutamaniraajitacharane Sukhade Shubhade Bhrityasharanye.||8||

Rogam Shokam Taapam Paapam Hara Me Bhagavati Kumatikalaapam.
Tribhuvanasaare Vasudhahaare Tvamasi Gatirmama Khalu Samsaare.||9||

Alakaanade Paramaanande Kuru Karunaamayi Kaataravandye.
Tava Tatanikatey Yasya Nivaasah Khalu Vaikunthe Tasya Nivaasah.||10||

Varamiha Neere Kamatho Meenah Kim Va Teere Sharatah Ksheenah.
Athava Shvapacho Malino Deenastava Na Hi Doore Nripatikuleenah.||11||

Bho Bhuvaneshvari Punye Dhanye Devi Dravamayi Munivarakanye.
Gangaastavamidam Amalam Nityam Pathati Naro Yah Sa Jayati Satyam.||12||

Yeshaam Hridaye Gangaabhaktisteshaam Bhavati Sadaa Sukhmuktih.
Madhurakaantaa Panjhatikabhih Paramaanandakalitalalitaabhih.

Gangaastotramidam Bhavasaaram Vaanchhitaphaladaam Viditamudaaram.
Shankaraacharyarachitam Pathanti Sajjanairmanasi Ye Dhritam.

|| Iti Shrimachhankaracharyavirachitam Gangaastotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) हे देवी, सुरेश्वरी, भगवती गंगे! तीनों लोकों को तारने वाली, चंचल तरंगों वाली, शंकर के मस्तक पर विहार करने वाली, विमल! मेरी बुद्धि आपके चरणकमलों में स्थिर रहे।

(2) हे भागीरथी! सुखदायिनी माता! आपके जल की महिमा वेदों में प्रसिद्ध है। मैं आपकी महिमा नहीं जानता, हे कृपामयी! इस अज्ञानी की रक्षा करो।

(3) हे हरि के चरणों के पाद्य-जल से प्रवाहित गंगे! हिम, चन्द्र और मुक्ता के समान धवल तरंगों वाली! मेरे दुष्कृत्यों का भार दूर करो, कृपया भवसागर से पार उतारो।

(4) आपका निर्मल जल जिसने पीया, उसने परमपद प्राप्त किया। हे माता गंगे! जो आपमें भक्त है, उसे देखने में यम भी समर्थ नहीं।

(5) हे पतितों का उद्धार करने वाली जाह्नवी गंगे! पर्वतराज को खण्डित करती हुई सुशोभित तरंगों वाली! भीष्म की जननी, मुनिवर की कन्ये! पतितों का निवारण करने वाली, त्रिभुवन में धन्य हो!

(6) लोक में कल्पलता के समान फल देने वाली, जो तुम्हें प्रणाम करता है वह शोक में नहीं गिरता।

(7) हे माता! यदि तुम्हारे स्रोत में स्नान किया, तो फिर वह गर्भ में नहीं जन्मता। हे नरक का निवारण करने वाली जाह्नवी! कलुष का नाश करने वाली, उत्तुंग महिमा वाली!

(8) हे पुण्य तरंगों वाली! जय जय हे जाह्नवी, करुणापूर्ण दृष्टि वाली! इन्द्र के मुकुट-मणि से राजित चरणों वाली, सुखदायिनी, शुभदायिनी, भक्तों की शरण्ये!

(9) रोग, शोक, ताप, पाप और कुमति का समूह हर लो, हे भगवती! तीनों लोकों की सार, पृथ्वी का हार, इस संसार में तुम ही मेरी गति हो।

(10) हे अलकानन्दे! परमानन्दे! हे करुणामयी! कातरों द्वारा वन्द्य! तुम्हारे तट के निकट जिसका निवास है, वस्तुतः वैकुण्ठ में उसका निवास है।

(11) यहाँ (गंगा के) जल में कछुआ या मछली होना श्रेष्ठ है, या तट पर क्षीण सरट (गिरगिट) होना, अथवा चाण्डाल मलिन दीन होना — आपसे दूर कुलीन राजा होने से अच्छा है।

(12) हे भुवनेश्वरी! पुण्य! धन्य! हे द्रवमयी देवी, मुनिवर कन्ये! यह गंगा स्तव निर्मल है — इसे जो नित्य पढ़ता है, वह सत्य ही जयी होता है।
benefits: Composed by Adi Shankaracharya - one of the holiest Ganga prayers
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and purifies the devotee completely
Liberates from the cycle of birth and death
Devotees of Ganga remain beyond the reach of Yama (death)
Invokes the merit of residing at ganga's banks
Invokes the blessing of moksha and removes all sorrows, diseases, and fears
description: The Ganga Stotram, known in Sanskrit as श्री गंगा स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Goddess Ganga. Rooted in Adi Shankaracharya, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to composed by Adi Shankaracharya - one of the holiest Ganga prayers as well as devotee of Ganga remain beyond the reach of Yama (death). Additionally, it is known to destroy all sins and purify the devotee completely. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Ganga Dussehra and Ganga Jayanti to invoke divine grace. At 12 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Ganga Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ganga Stotram by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn praising Goddess Ganga for purification, liberation and removal of sins.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ganga Stotram and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Stotram is a Sanskrit hymn in praise of Goddess Ganga, the divine river-goddess and most sacred of all Hindu tirthas. The most famous version is attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (8th century CE) and begins with the celebrated verse Devi Sureshvari Bhagavati Gange, Tribhuvana-tarini taraladeha — O Goddess Sureshvari, Bhagavati Ganga, liberator of the three worlds. The hymn praises Ganga as the daughter of Himavan, sister of Parvati, descender from Vishnu's feet, captured in Shiva's matted locks, and brought to earth by King Bhagiratha's tapasya. Each verse extols her purifying power and seeks her grace for moksha. The shorter Ganga Ashtakam and the longer Ganga Stotram of Shankara are both widely recited. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya granthavali; Ramayana Bala Kanda; Hindupedia Ganga.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Ganga and what is her descent story?
seoFaqs.answer: Goddess Ganga is the divine river-goddess, born from the kamandalu of Brahma after he washed Vishnu's feet during the Vamana avatara, hence flowing from the toe of Vishnu (Vishnu-padodbhava). Originally a celestial river in heaven (akasha-ganga or Mandakini), she descended to earth through the tapasya of King Bhagiratha (her epithet Bhagirathi), who sought to liberate his ancestors burned to ashes by the curse of Sage Kapila. Knowing that her direct fall would shatter the earth, Shiva caught her in his matted locks (jata) and released her gently in seven streams. From the Himalayan glacier at Gomukh she flows 2525 km across North India to merge with the Bay of Bengal at Ganga Sagar. (Sources: Ramayana Bala Kanda; Mahabharata Anushasana Parva; Hindupedia Ganga.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Ganga considered the most sacred river in Hinduism?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga is regarded as the most sacred river because tradition holds that her waters carry direct purifying potency from the highest divine source — having flowed from Vishnu's feet, through Brahma's kamandalu, through Shiva's locks, brought down by Bhagiratha's twelve-thousand-year tapasya. Bathing in the Ganga (Ganga-snana) is said to wash away the karmic accumulation of multiple lifetimes, drinking her water (Ganga-jala) is the supreme antidote to the gravest sins, and dying with her water on one's lips guarantees Vaikuntha. Modern science has confirmed unique properties of Ganga water including high dissolved oxygen, bacteriophages that prevent putrefaction, and self-cleansing capacity. The river hosts the largest religious gatherings on earth — the Kumbh Melas. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Bhagavata Purana; Hindupedia Ganga.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Ganga Stotram be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The most powerful occasions are Ganga Dussehra (Jyeshtha Shukla Dashami, May-June, the day Ganga descended to earth from heaven), Ganga Saptami / Ganga Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Saptami, when she emerged from Shiva's locks), the four Kumbh Melas at Haridwar, Prayagraj, Nashik and Ujjain (12-year cycle), Makar Sankranti (Ganga Sagar mela), Kartik Purnima (Dev Diwali at Kashi), Maha-Shivaratri at Haridwar, every Sunday and Ekadashi, and at the start of any Ganga-snana or Char Dham Yatra. Daily recitation while taking morning bath (visualising the Ganga descending into one's bathing vessel) is the prescribed sadhana even for those far from the river. (Sources: Drikpanchang Ganga Dussehra; Hindupedia Ganga utsavas; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the most sacred Ganga tirthas and ghats?
seoFaqs.answer: The most sacred Ganga tirthas, from her Himalayan source to the sea, are: Gomukh (the glacial source in Uttarakhand), Gangotri (the first temple-shrine), Devprayag (where Bhagirathi and Alakananda meet to become the Ganga proper), Rishikesh (Triveni Ghat), Haridwar (Har-Ki-Pauri ghat — the principal Kumbh Mela site, Brahma-kund), Garhmukteshwar, Soron, Kanpur, Prayagraj (Triveni Sangam — confluence of Ganga, Yamuna and the invisible Saraswati, the second Kumbh Mela site), Kashi-Varanasi (the supreme moksha-tirtha with the famous ghats including Dashashwamedh, Manikarnika and Harishchandra), Patna, Bhagalpur, Murshidabad and Ganga Sagar (the island where she meets the Bay of Bengal, site of the Sagar mela on Makar Sankranti). (Sources: Skanda Purana Kashi Khanda; Hindupedia Ganga tirthas; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Ganga aarti at Haridwar and Varanasi?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Aarti is the daily evening worship of Mother Ganga performed by priests on the river ghats with elaborate brass lamps (panchadeepas), conch-blowing, drumming and Sanskrit chanting. The most famous is the Har-Ki-Pauri aarti at Haridwar's Brahma-kund (the spot where Vishnu's footprint and a drop of amrita are said to have fallen), held every sunset since centuries. The Dashashwamedh Ghat aarti at Varanasi (held simultaneously by seven young pandits in synchronised choreography) is the second most famous, with the Subah-e-Banaras dawn aarti also drawing large crowds. The Ganga Stotram is recited as part of the aarti liturgy, and devotees float diyas (deepa-daan) in leaf-bowls on the river. (Sources: Haridwar Tirtha Purohit Sabha; Kashi Vidvat Parishad; Hindupedia Ganga aarti.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ganga Stotram promise to its reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that regular recitation of the Ganga Stotram grants the merit equivalent to bathing in the actual Ganga even when far from the river, purification from the gravest sins (mahapatakas including brahma-hatya, sura-pana, steya, guru-talpa-gama), removal of accumulated karma from this and previous lifetimes, healing from chronic skin diseases and digestive disorders (Ganga-jala being the traditional cure), freedom from progeny obstacles, success in pitr-tarpana (ancestor-offerings), the moksha-tirtha-phala of Kashi (since the stotra makes any place a Ganga-tirtha), protection during pilgrimage travel, and ultimately Vaikuntha or Kailasa-prapti at the time of death. The Skanda Purana promises that one who recites this stotra daily attains the same fruit as bathing at all Ganga tirthas in succession. (Sources: Adi Shankara Ganga Stotram phalashruti; Skanda Purana; Hindupedia Ganga upasana.)
